source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 3. 4차 CE 인허가/Attachment 10. Clinical Evaluation/Attachment 10-5 Post Market Surveillance Report/Post Market Surveillance Report [Doc.No.A-PMS-004].docx

document.title: Post Market Surveillance Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Post Market Surveillance Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector summarizing PMS activities, similar-device adverse event and recall searches, PMS results, FSCA/FSN planning, and PMCF assessment.
sourceSummary.officialUse: The report states that its information is intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies of H&abyz Co. Ltd.
keyPoints: PMS was conducted in accordance with PMS Plan Doc. No. A-PMS-003.
ADD is described as a Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children, not for mammography.
The device is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
PMS information sources selected from NB-MED-2.12 / 1 were literature reviews, media, and experience with similar devices.
The report concludes that no additional measures such as product design change and process improvement are required.
The report states that PMCF does not need to be performed.

evidence.claim: The document is a Post Market Surveillance Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector.

The report was prepared, reviewed, and approved on Jan/29/2021.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared Sales team No, Jungmin Jan/29/2021; Review R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin Jan/29/2021; Approval Quality Manager Hur, Namkyu Jan/29/2021

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd. in Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacture H&abyz Co., Ltd. 1F, 2-dong, 41-16, cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-myeon, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea

evidence.claim: ADD is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis and treatment planning on a real time basis for physicians and radiologists. Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The device is classified as Class IIa under MDD 93/42/EEC Annex IX Rule 16 as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

evidence.claim: PMS activities used literature reviews, media, and experience with similar devices as sources.
evidence.evidenceText: To collect data, we selected three sources from the PMS sources listed in 'NB-MED-2.12 / 1'. 1. Literature Reviews 2. The media 3. Experience with similar devices made by the same or different manufacturer

evidence.claim: The literature search covered publications from 2000 to 2019 and was searched from 2020.01.05 to 2020.06.15.
evidence.evidenceText: Date of Search 2020.01.05 ~ 2020.06.15; Period Covered by Search From 2000 to 2019 years

evidence.claim: The literature sources listed were PUBMED, European Radiology, and Medical Physics.
evidence.evidenceText: Literature Source PUBMED, European Radiology, Medical Physics

evidence.claim: The media search found no applicable results.
evidence.evidenceText: We searched as keyword such as ... in google and yahoo, it is not found applicable

evidence.claim: Korea, Europe, and USA similar-device experience searches were conducted on 2020.08.05.
evidence.evidenceText: Nation Search Date Korea ... 2020.08.05 Europe ... 2020.08.05 USA ... 2020.08.05

evidence.claim: The Korea search concluded no adverse event or recall related to the Digital Flat Panel X-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: There is no adverse event or recall related to the Digital Flat Panel X-ray detector in the Korea

evidence.claim: The USA MAUDE review identified one similar-device battery event and referred it to the Clinical Evaluation Report.
evidence.evidenceText: This event is related to similar device. It is explained in clinical evaluation report Cl)4. Refer to Clinical Evaluation Report (Doc.No. A-TCF-5-060)

evidence.claim: The PMS result concluded no additional product design change or process improvement was required.
evidence.evidenceText: As a result of the post-2020.06 market research analysis, no additional measures such as product design change and process improvement are required.

evidence.claim: The report states that FSCA will be implemented for serious adverse events and accidents with the product in accordance with MEDDEV 2.12 Post-Market Surveillance.
evidence.evidenceText: In the event of serious adverse events and an accident with our product, Field Safety Corrective Action will be implemented in accordance with MEDDEV 2.12 Post-Market Surveillance.

evidence.claim: The report states that PMCF does not need to be performed.
evidence.evidenceText: But all the cases are not met to PMS activity data. So, the PMCF does not need to be performed.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the document classification and FSCA/FSN framework cite MDD 93/42/EEC, Directive 2007/47/EEC, NB-MED-2.12 / 1, and MEDDEV 2.12, while PMS searches also cover Korea, Europe, and USA.

PMCF table contains apparently conflicting device wording: 'Our device is a product that pushes the limbs and helps the blood to circulate well,' while the device information identifies ADD as a Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector.
Document number in sourcePath is Doc.No.A-PMS-004, while the report title page states Report No.: A-TCF-5-000 (Rev.1); both identifiers are preserved where applicable.
The document date is inferred from prepared/review/approval date and revision 1 date of Jan/29/2021; no separate single effective date field is present.
